perturbation.original_text: The consideration for the covenants and agreements of each PARTY contained in this AGREEMENT shall be that PARTY’s right to receive access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION as part of NRSC analysis, and that PARTY’s right to protect such CONF IDENTIAL INFORMATION as it deems appropriate to disclose pursuant to this AGREEMENT, which the PARTIES acknowledge and agree shall constitute sufficient and adequate consideration.
perturbation.changed_text: The consideration for the covenants and agreements of each PARTY contained in this AGREEMENT shall be that MEMBER's right to receive access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION as part of NRSC analysis, and that PARTY’s right to protect such CONF IDENTIAL INFORMATION as it deems appropriate to disclose pursuant to this AGREEMENT, which the PARTIES acknowledge and agree shall constitute sufficient and adequate consideration.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'PARTY' to 'MEMBER' in the beginning of Section 2, while keeping 'PARTY' later in the same paragraph and throughout the document, introduces ambiguity. 'MEMBER' is not defined, so it creates uncertainty about whose rights are being considered (members vs. all parties) and creates an internal contradiction. The contradiction arises from the text implying that 'MEMBER' has rights while the rest of the agreement relies on 'PARTY' being the binding entity. This creates uncertainty about which entities are subject to those rights, and creates intext contradictions within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The consideration for the covenants and agreements of each PARTY contained in this AGREEMENT shall be that MEMBER's right to receive access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION as part of NRSC analysis, and that PARTY’s right to protect such CONF IDENTIAL INFORMATION as it deems appropriate to disclose pursuant to this AGREEMENT, which the PARTIES acknowledge and agree shall constitute sufficient and adequate consideration.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'PARTY' to 'MEMBER' at the beginning of the section, while retaining 'PARTY' later in the same section and throughout the document, introduces a strong in-text contradiction. As explained, 'MEMBER' is undefined, creating ambiguity about whose rights are being considered. This directly conflicts with the rest of the agreement which relies on 'PARTY' as the binding entity, thus creating uncertainty about the entities subject to those rights. The changed and contradicted text both refer to the same section, highlighting the direct contradiction caused by the inconsistent terminology, which aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: (c) The PARTIES hereby agree that the DISCLOSING PARTY shall retain ownership and control over any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed pursuant to this AGREEMENT. The RECEIVING PARTIES shall not by reason of any disclosure made pursuant to this AGREEMENT thereby obtain any rights with respect to such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than the right of the PARTY, or an ENTITY that the PARTY assigns its rights to under this Agreement in compliance with Section 5 herein, to use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purposes of NRSC analysis only, and agree that they will not: (i) use such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for any 2 reason other than NRSC analysis only as expressly authorized by this Agreement; or (ii) sell, lend, lease, distribute, license, give, transfer, assign, show, disclose, disseminate, appropriate, or otherwise communicate any such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any person or ENTITY outside the RECEIVING PARTY or its assignee under this Agreement, except as expressly authorized under this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) The PARTIES hereby agree that the DISCLOSING PARTY shall retain ownership and control over any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed pursuant to this AGREEMENT. The RECEIVING PARTIES shall not by reason of any disclosure made pursuant to this AGREEMENT thereby obtain any rights with respect to such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than the right of the ORGANIZATION, or an ENTITY that the PARTY assigns its rights to under this Agreement in compliance with Section 5 herein, to use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purposes of NRSC analysis only, and agree that they will not: (i) use such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for any 2 reason other than NRSC analysis only as expressly authorized by this Agreement; or (ii) sell, lend, lease, distribute, license, give, transfer, assign, show, disclose, disseminate, appropriate, or otherwise communicate any such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any person or ENTITY outside the RECEIVING PARTY or its assignee under this Agreement, except as expressly authorized under this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'PARTY' to 'ORGANIZATION' the RECEIVING PARTIES are unsure whether that is the organization that the party represents or not. The term 'ORGANIZATION' is undefined and replacing 'PARTY' with it creates confusion. This creates an internal contradiction because it's unclear which entity ('PARTY' or 'ORGANIZATION') can use the confidential information. This lack of clarity undermines the purpose of the NDA.
perturbation.location: Section 3. Nondisclosure; Ownership of Proprietary Property
perturbation.contradicted_location: Section 3. Nondisclosure; Ownership of Proprietary Property
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) The PARTIES hereby agree that the DISCLOSING PARTY shall retain ownership and control over any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed pursuant to this AGREEMENT. The RECEIVING PARTIES shall not by reason of any disclosure made pursuant to this AGREEMENT thereby obtain any rights with respect to such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than the right of the ORGANIZATION, or an ENTITY that the PARTY assigns its rights to under this Agreement in compliance with Section 5 herein, to use the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purposes of NRSC analysis only, and agree that they will not: (i) use such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for any 2 reason other than NRSC analysis only as expressly authorized by this Agreement; or (ii) sell, lend, lease, distribute, license, give, transfer, assign, show, disclose, disseminate, appropriate, or otherwise communicate any such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any person or ENTITY outside the RECEIVING PARTY or its assignee under this Agreement, except as expressly authorized under this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'PARTY' to 'ORGANIZATION' introduces ambiguity regarding who can use the confidential information. The original text clearly defines the 'PARTY' having rights, but the changed text uses 'ORGANIZATION,' which is undefined and could refer to an entity different from the 'PARTY.' This uncertainty creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether the rights granted to the 'PARTY' also apply to the 'ORGANIZATION,' or if the 'ORGANIZATION' has separate rights. This is a clear contradiction based on Misaligned Terminology.
